user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
kurtosis refers to the tailedness of the distribution, meaning how fat the tails are.
in a negative skewed distribution, we have left-tail,
while in a positive skewed distribution we have right-tail
comment.raw_body:
kurtosis refers to the tailedness of the distribution, meaning how fat the tails are.
in a negative skewed distribution, we have left-tail,
while in a positive skewed distribution we have right-tail

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
if vomma is bid, the tail is fat, bcs volatility is expected to jump higher, meaning more illiquidity.
if vomma is supplied, the tail becomes thin, bcs volatility is expected to be "braked" (no acceleration, flat convexity), meaning less illiquidity
comment.raw_body:
if vomma is bid, the tail is fat, bcs volatility is expected to jump higher, meaning more illiquidity.
if vomma is supplied, the tail becomes thin, bcs volatility is expected to be "braked" (no acceleration, flat convexity), meaning less illiquidity

comment.body:
basic calculation:
take the 22 delta put IV, distract 22 delta call IV, and divide it by the ATM IV
and voilá, you got the skew.
(25 delta also can be used, i like 22 delta better bcs of catching the highest vanna)

user.name: Alma

comment.body:
if you think about this:
higher skew = more put demand or less call demand = short upside vol, long downside vol
lower skew = less put demand or more call demand = long upside vol, short downside vol

relatively!

comment.body:
now lets say you long the skew (long OTM put, short OTM call),
now the IV you sold through the calls is lower than the one you bought through the put. 
usually the delta is neutralized in order to trade only the vanna.

comment.body:
(delta neutrality requires very similar counter trade as in the case of MM hegding their gamma trade)
spot/vol beta is negative, and i short the skew, meaning I have positive vanna = spot down/vol up/delta up, I sell futs against the delta exposure, 
spot up/vol down/delta down = I buy futs
